Web search results for query: "cryptocurrency momentum factor cross-sectional 2024 2025 liu tsyvinski replication"

Links: [{"title":"Cryptocurrency momentum has (not) its moments | Financial Markets and Portfolio Management | Springer Nature Link","url":"https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s11408-025-00474-9"},{"title":"Common Risk Factors in Cryptocurrency by Yukun Liu, Aleh Tsyvinski, Xi Wu :: SSRN","url":"https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=3379131"},{"title":"A Trend Factor for the Cross Section of Cryptocurrency ...","url":"https://www.cambridge.org/core/services/aop-cambridge-core/content/view/4C1509ACBA33D5DCAF0AC24379148178/S0022109024000747a.pdf/trend_factor_for_the_cross_section_of_cryptocurrency_returns.pdf"},{"title":"Time-Series and Cross-Sectional Momentum in the Cryptocurrency Market: A Comprehensive Analysis under Realistic Assumptions","url":"https://www.researchgate.net/publication/377457967_Time-Series_and_Cross-Sectional_Momentum_in_the_Cryptocurrency_Market_A_Comprehensive_Analysis_under_Realistic_Assumptions"},{"title":"Dynamic time series momentum of cryptocurrencies - ScienceDirect","url":"https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S1062940821000590"},{"title":"Cryptocurrency as an Investable Asset Class: Coming of Age","url":"https://arxiv.org/html/2510.14435v1"},{"title":"Cryptocurrency Market Risk-Managed Momentum Strategies","url":"https://www.researchgate.net/publication/393284396_Cryptocurrency_Market_Risk-Managed_Momentum_Strategies"},{"title":"Common Risk Factors in Cryptocurrency - LIU - 2022 - The Journal of Finance - Wiley Online Library","url":"https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/abs/10.1111/jofi.13119"},{"title":"[PDF] Risks and Returns of Cryptocurrency | Semantic Scholar","url":"https://www.semanticscholar.org/paper/Risks-and-Returns-of-Cryptocurrency-Liu-Tsyvinski/2a70cf6787c84fbb15632bf3986d60094c71da69"},{"title":"Common Risk Factors in Cryptocurrency","url":"https://www.researchgate.net/publication/358555085_Common_Risk_Factors_in_Cryptocurrency"}]

## Search Results Summary

I found several recent and relevant research articles on cryptocurrency momentum factors and cross-sectional returns. Here are the key findings:

### Core Finding on the Three-Factor Model

Three factors – cryptocurrency market, size, and momentum – capture the cross-sectional expected cryptocurrency returns. This foundational finding comes from Liu, Tsyvinski, and Wu's research that has been widely cited and extended.

### Recent 2024-2025 Research on Momentum

A March 2025 article published in Financial Markets and Portfolio Management provides a critical reassessment of cryptocurrency momentum. The main results derived from using a sample of large-cap cryptocurrencies and equal-weighted momentum portfolios indicate that cryptocurrency momentum is subject to severe crashes. 

More specifically, volatility management is a useful tool for mitigating cryptocurrency momentum crashes. The research also noted that cryptocurrency momentum appears to be a phenomenon associated with large-cap cryptocurrencies.

### Notable Recent Work (2024-2025)

Borri, Chetverikov, Liu, and Tsyvinski published "One factor to bind the cross-section of returns" in April 2024, and they also have a 2025 Yale Working Paper on forward selection Fama-MacBeth regression with higher order asset-pricing factors.

These recent studies suggest that while the Liu-Tsyvinski momentum factor framework remains influential for explaining cryptocurrency returns, the robustness and reliability of momentum strategies has been questioned in subsequent research, particularly regarding tail risks and large drawdowns.

I've completed a web search on perpetual futures funding rates, basis, and crypto exchanges. Here's what I found:

## Key Findings

**What are Funding Rates?**

Funding rates are the periodic payments exchanged between long and short traders in perpetual futures markets. Since perpetual contracts have no expiry date, funding is the mechanism that keeps the contract price aligned with the underlying asset price and makes divergence costly.

**How They Work:**

Funding is a payment made from one side of the trade to the other. When the funding rate is negative, shorts pay longs, when funding is positive longs pay shorts.

**2024-2025 Market Data:**

Bitcoin's aggregate funding rate was overwhelmingly positive through 2024, spending only 26 days negative while BTC more than doubled and reached a new all-time high in December. However, positive funding means longs are crowded enough that they are paying a premium to maintain exposure and this can persist for surprisingly long periods in strong trends.

**Exchange Information:**

The search results confirm that both Binance's developer documentation confirms dedicated endpoints for funding history and funding info in the USDⓈ-M futures API, and Deribit provides BTC Funding Rate data for BTC-PERPETUAL contracts with real-time and historical data.

Here are the search results for your query on commodity futures convenience yield and predictability:

## Key Findings:

**Convenience Yield and Return Predictability:**

Out-of-sample forecasts show that convenience yields and the de-trended real oil price may have more predictive power with respect to future spot prices than interest and exchange rates within 1–12 months.

**Recent 2024-2025 Research:**

A recent study examines the predictability of commodity futures returns using image-based price patterns extracted from open-high-low-close (OHLC) charts. Additionally, machine learning models have been used to investigate the predictability of futures returns in 22 commodities with commodity-specific and macroeconomic factors as predictors.

**Convenience Yield Theory:**

When inventories are low, futures markets move into backwardation, and holding physical commodities earns a "convenience yield." When inventories are high, futures curves slope upward into contango, signaling weaker returns.

**Convenience Yield Risk as Predictor:**

A significant cross-sectional relationship exists between the convenience yield and future commodity returns, and "carry" (related to the convenience yield) predicts commodity returns in the time series and cross-section. The convenience yield risk signal positively predicts commodity returns.

The search results indicate active research into commodity futures predictability, with convenience yields being recognized as an important predictor of commodity returns across oil, gold, copper, and other commodities.

Based on the search results, here's what I found regarding CDX HY CDS credit spreads and equity volatility in 2024-2025:

## CDX HY Credit Spread Movements

In July 2024, with resurgent recession fears after a weak jobs report and equity market volatility, CDX HY spreads widened out to the mid to high 300s. More recently, credit spreads widened consistently during the last month of the first quarter, with US high yield spreads (CDX HY) adding approximately 70 bps to end March 2025 at 376 bps.

## Intraday Volatility

The search results show that the CDX/Cboe NA High Yield 6-Month Volatility Index seeks to track the market's expectation of the range of movement in the CDX North American High Yield Index five-year spreads over the next six months, indicating active interest in measuring spread volatility.

## Equity-Credit Dynamics

The Quality-Junk overlay has a high correlation with credit spread widening even as the higher yield and lower volatility of these equities have historically generated a positive carry, suggesting an empirical relationship between equity quality factors and credit spread movements.

## Liquidity Advantage of CDS

Notably, CDS indices offer bid-ask spreads 10 times lower than corporate bond indices in the HY space, which would be relevant for analyzing intraday lead-lag relationships between these markets.

The search results focus more on spread levels and management strategies than on specific intraday lead-lag analysis between equity volatility and CDS spreads.

Lee∗ Minho Wang†","url":"https://www.scheller.gatech.edu/directory/research/finance/lee/pdf/leewang17.pdf"},{"title":"BIS Bulletin No 90 The market turbulence and carry trade unwind of August 2024","url":"https://www.bis.org/publ/bisbull90.pdf"},{"title":"Measuring liquidity risk effects on carry trades across currencies and regimes - ScienceDirect","url":"https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1042444X21000074"},{"title":"Carry trades and risk factors heterogeneity: Three asymmetries - ScienceDirect","url":"https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0165176525006159"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found regarding FX carry trade crash risk, CIP deviations, and high-frequency intraday dynamics for 2024-2025:

## Recent Carry Trade Unwind (August 2024)

In early August 2024, financial markets faced sharp volatility as large-scale carry trades began to unwind, with the episode peaking on August 5, when Japan's TOPIX index plunged 12% in a single day, and the Volatility Index (VIX) spiked to levels not seen since the COVID-19 pandemic. Once volatility rises or central banks shift tone, carry positions are typically the first to unwind.

## Funding Risk and CIP Deviations

Funding constraints in international currency markets are measured by deviations in the covered interest rate parity, with funding risk driven by conditions in the financial sector of low interest rate carry trade countries, oil price volatility, and central bank actions. Funding risk has been relevant in currency carry trading after 2008, suggesting that investors' funding constraints start binding at that time.

## Crash Risk Compensation

Higher crash risk significantly increases expected returns, and Shapley decomposition attributes over 20% of the explained variance in expected carry returns to crash risk, while balance sheet constraints and global risk aversion further reinforce premia.

## High-Frequency Intraday Dynamics

Approximately 1.5% of observations represent intraday jumps, implying that roughly ten intraday jumps occur per week. Research has documented substantial intraday volatility patterns in FX markets relevant to carry trade positions.

I've performed a web search for your query on the Amihud illiquidity measure, intraday liquidity premium, and US equity markets for 2024-2025. Here are the key findings:

## Overview of the Amihud Illiquidity Measure

The Amihud (2002) measure is one of the most widely used liquidity proxies in the finance literature, with over one hundred and twenty papers published in major journals during 2009-2015 using the Amihud measure for empirical analyses. The Amihud measure uses the absolute value of the daily return-to-volume ratio to capture price impact.

## Recent Research on Intraday Liquidity Measures

Recent academic work has focused on improving the Amihud measure for intraday applications. A novel liquidity measure was introduced that addresses two key limitations of the traditional Amihud's measure by excluding overnight returns and incorporating the recency effect in the Taiwan stock market. The time-weighted daytime Amihud's illiquidity measure exhibits a more pronounced positive effect on subsequent aggregate market returns relative to Amihud's illiquidity measure.

## The Illiquidity Premium

There is a positive (risk-adjusted) mean of the excess return of illiquid over liquid stocks. However, research has questioned the components driving this premium. The transitory half-Amihud measure on the days of negative permanent price returns plays an important role in pricing the Amihud illiquidity, with findings suggesting that both the trading volume component and transitory price impact drive the Amihud illiquidity premium.

The search results focus primarily on academic literature and research from 2021-2024, with the most recent empirical evidence coming from studies on the Taiwan market rather than specifically focused US equity research for 2024-2025.

I've completed the web search for your query. Here's a summary of the findings on news sentiment analysis using LLMs and FinBERT for stock return prediction in 2024-2025:

## Key Research Findings

**FinBERT-LSTM Models:**
Research has advanced FinBERT models by incorporating Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) architecture, utilizing news categories related to market, industry, and stock-related news combined with stock price data for prediction. Studies trained on NASDAQ-100 index data and Benzinga news articles showed that FinBERT-LSTM performs the best, followed by LSTM, with DNN models ranking third.

**ChatGPT vs. FinBERT:**
Research shows that ChatGPT can predict stock returns based on news headlines. ChatGPT 3.5's prediction score has a statistically significant relation with next-day stock returns, with a switch from negative to positive prediction associated with a 51.8 basis points increase in next-day stock return.

**Advanced LLM Approaches:**
Recent frameworks combine FinBERT-based financial sentiment extraction with technical and statistical indicators to forecast short-term stock price movement. Trading strategies based on fine-tuned LLaMA-2 sentiments produce significantly higher buy-and-hold returns compared to FinBERT, with LLaMA-2 sentiment signals showing strong correlation with cumulative abnormal returns.

**Intraday Trading Applications:**
ChatGPT's zero-shot NLP capabilities have been utilized to analyze daily U.S. news summaries and generate market sentiment indicators for short-term stock return forecasting.

The research indicates that sentiment-based prediction models are evolving rapidly, with newer LLMs showing improved performance over traditional FinBERT approaches, particularly for short-term and intraday trading signals.

Implications for price discovery and liquidity - ScienceDirect","url":"https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S1386418123000502"},{"title":"SPY Historical Stock Price Data","url":"https://stockanalysis.com/etf/spy/history/"}]

I've completed the web search for your query about closing auction, open auction, SPY intraday return predictability, and E-mini contracts. Here are the key findings:

## Intraday Return Predictability

Research on intraday market return predictability examined 15-minute returns on the Fama-French market portfolio from 1996 to 2020, and the overall highest out-of-sample R² of 0.212% was obtained using ensemble machine learning methods.

## Closing Auction Dynamics

Closing auctions are used to establish the official end-of-day price by matching buy and sell interest at a single clearing price. Recent analysis shows that auction volume and analysis metrics changed dramatically during the closing auction in the 2024 Russell Reconstitution.

Closing auctions set daily closing prices for U.S. stocks and account for a striking 7.5% of daily volume in 2018, up from 3.1% in 2010. Auction price deviations revert quickly and almost completely, on average.

## SPY and E-mini Relationship

The SPY (SPDR S&P 500 ETF) tends to closely follow the movements of the ES (E-mini S&P 500 futures contract), though the CME contract is traded 23-hours on CME, so the close and open are very different from SPY.

## Return Predictability from Closing Imbalances

The price impact from large market-on-close order imbalances is economically large and transitory, leading to short-term price reversal, with a long/short trading strategy exploiting this reversal resulting in a significant risk-adjusted return of 13.2 basis points per day.

Here are the search results for your query on macro announcement premium, intraday FOMC CPI returns, and volatility for 2024-2025:

## Key Findings:

**FOMC and CPI Impact on Volatility:**
Implied volatility of the S&P 100 index rises before scheduled news and decreases after FOMC, PPI and CPI announcements. Additionally, VIX consistently decreases on FOMC meeting days, regardless of the meeting's content.

**Intraday Trading Premium Around Announcements:**
Research shows significant intraday market activity around major macro announcements. Following FOMC releases, the VIX spot index and VIX futures exhibit significant declines with a sharp increase in VIX futures trading volume, and trading strategies capitalizing on this observed pattern yield an average return of 10%.

**Macroeconomic Drivers of Intraday Volatility:**
Federal Reserve target interest rate decisions and non-farm payroll announcements are the most significant drivers of intraday jumps, and CPI, trade balance, non-farm payroll, FOMC decisions, retail sales, business inventories, and personal income announcement surprises are significant factors impacting intraday currency jumps.

**Overall Impact of Economic Announcements:**
Central bank interest rate decisions, Non-Farm Payrolls (NFP), GDP releases, the Consumer Price Index (CPI), and unemployment data are high-impact events that can cause significant volatility across forex, equities, commodities, and bond markets.

These findings indicate substantial "macro announcement premiums" exist around FOMC and CPI releases, with elevated intraday volatility and trading opportunities around these scheduled events in 2024-2025.

I've completed the web search for your query about gold, silver, oil, realized volatility, HAR forecasting models, and commodity futures for 2024-2025. Here's a summary of the key findings:

## Research Findings

### HAR Model Applications for Commodity Volatility Forecasting

Building on the heterogeneous autoregressive (HAR) model, research shows that models incorporating variables that map the equity channel, sentiment, or foreign exchange (FX) market volatility result in more accurate predictions of realized volatility than the baseline HAR. Researchers have developed extensions labeled HAR-Equity, HAR-Sentiment, HAR-Commodity, HAR-FX, and HAR-Google for forecasting crude oil futures volatility.

HAR models can compensate for defects in traditional ARMA and VAR approaches and have attracted in-depth studies due to their high forecasting accuracy.

### 2024-2025 Commodity Market Performance

**Gold:** The accumulation trend didn't slow in 2024-2025; it accelerated. Gold made significant moves in 2024, with the yellow metal hitting an all-time high of over $2,800 in November.

The search returned relevant information about analyst forecast revisions, PEAD (Post-Earnings Announcement Drift), and IBES data. Here are the key findings:

## I/B/E/S Analyst Forecasts

I/B/E/S provides historical earnings estimates from analysts for both U.S. and international companies, and includes buy-hold-sell recommendations. The Institutional Brokers' Estimate System (I/B/E/S) was founded by the New York brokerage firm Lynch, Jones & Ryan and Technimetrics, Inc., and began collecting earnings estimates for U.S. companies around 1976.

## PEAD and Analyst Forecast Revisions

Recent evidence confirms a multi-quarter but declining PEAD. Studies have built on IBES analyst forecasts, and the magnitude of the drift is greater using analyst forecasts compared to time-series predictions. Forecast-based earnings surprise measures yield greater and more persistent abnormal returns than those based on seasonal random walk models.

The search results primarily contain academic research on PEAD and information about IBES data resources, rather than specific 2024-2025 intraday studies or findings. For more current research on intraday analyst forecast revisions and single-stock PEAD patterns in 2024-2025, you may need to access academic databases or specialized financial research platforms directly.

Based on the search results, here's what research reveals about retail investor order flow, sentiment, and stock returns in the 2024-2025 period:

## Key Research Findings

**Retail Order Flow Patterns:**
Aggregate measures of retail order flow have been shown to be contrarian in nature and predictive of future short-term stock returns, though the effectiveness varies by investor sophistication.

**Sentiment as a Predictor:**
More individual stock attention leads to more stock purchases, and sentiment is a contrarian predictor of future returns. However, when attention herds on a stock with high user engagement, trades peak but there is no reversal in returns.

**Robinhood Investor Behavior:**
Recent research shows that sentiment-driven and risk-seeking trading behaviors of Robinhood investors lead to overreactions to event-related news, with Robinhood investors exhibiting a remarkably lower level of investment sophistication.

**Market Quality Impact:**
For stocks with high retail interest, negative shocks to Robinhood investor participation are associated with reduced market order imbalances, increased market liquidity, and lower return volatility, suggesting that inexperienced retail trading can create liquidity challenges.

The research indicates that while retail sentiment and order flow have predictive value, the relationship is nuanced—sentiment acts as a contrarian signal for returns, and herding behavior among less sophisticated investors may limit traditional reversal patterns.

A cross-asset perspective | World Gold Council","url":"https://www.gold.org/goldhub/research/why-gold-2026-cross-asset-perspective"},{"title":"Navigating cross-asset volatility in turbulent times | LSEG","url":"https://www.lseg.com/en/insights/data-analytics/navigating-cross-asset-volatility-in-turbulent-times"},{"title":"Modelling time-varying volatility spillovers across crises: Evidence from major commodity futures and the US stock market - ScienceDirect","url":"https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0140988325000489"},{"title":"Cross-asset time-series momentum: Crude oil volatility and global stock markets - ScienceDirect","url":"https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0378426622002849"},{"title":"Stock–Commodity Correlations, Optimal Hedging, and Climate Risks - Demiralay - 2025 - Journal of Futures Markets - Wiley Online Library","url":"https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1002/fut.70014"},{"title":"The Monetary Policy–Commodities Nexus: A Survey - Bohl - Journal of Economic Surveys - Wiley Online Library","url":"https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/full/10.1111/joes.70025"},{"title":"Cross-Asset Correlations in Market Turbulence - PGIM","url":"https://www.pgim.com/sg/en/institutional/insights/asset-class/multi-asset/quantitative-solutions/cross-asset-correlations-constructing-portfolios-amid-market-turbulence"}]

I've found several relevant research papers and articles on cross-asset volatility spillovers involving equities, bonds, and commodities from 2024-2025. Here are the key findings:

## Recent Research on Volatility Spillovers (2024-2025)

**Realized Volatility and Market Interconnections:**

Research published in 2024 examines spillover between social media sentiment and market volatility among stock, bond, foreign exchange, and commodity markets. Information mainly flows from volatility to sentiment indices, with the VIX being the most significant net transmitter.

**Specific Cross-Asset Patterns:**

One study indicates that the S&P 500 is the largest net transmitter of volatility information, predominantly affecting crude oil, heating oil, and gold futures, with spillovers intensifying during crises. Gold futures receive heightened volatility transmissions during crises, alluding to the "flight to quality" characteristic displayed by investors.

**2024-2025 Market Conditions:**

On a rolling 52-week basis, correlations turned significantly positive in 2022 in the United States and United Kingdom, peaking at 50% in July 2024 and 63% in June 2024, respectively.

**Recent 2025 Volatility Events:**

In April 2025, following a wave of tariff announcements from the Trump administration, global markets experienced significant turbulence, with nearly $6.6 trillion wiped from U.S. stock market value over two trading days, only for the S&P 500 to rebound by 9.5% the following day. Spillover effects continue to rattle European equity markets and impact commodity prices, creating a knock-on effect across currencies and asset classes.

The search results show active recent research on these cross-asset dynamics, though most focus on correlations and volatility transmission mechanisms rather than strictly realized volatility measures.

Kelly, Asaf Manela :: SSRN","url":"https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=2721783"},{"title":"Intermediary Asset Pricing: New Evidence from Many Asset Classes | NBER","url":"https://www.nber.org/papers/w21920"},{"title":"Intermediary Asset Pricing: New Evidence from Many Asset Classes","url":"https://ideas.repec.org/p/nbr/nberwo/21920.html"},{"title":"Intermediary asset pricing: New evidence from many asset classes - ScienceDirect","url":"https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0304405X1730212X"},{"title":"NBER WORKING PAPER SERIES INTERMEDIARY ASSET PRICING:","url":"https://www.nber.org/system/files/working_papers/w21920/w21920.pdf"},{"title":"Intermediary asset pricing: New evidence from many asset classes | Request PDF","url":"https://www.researchgate.net/publication/319089503_Intermediary_asset_pricing_New_evidence_from_many_asset_classes"},{"title":"Intermediary Capital Ratio and Risk Factor – Zhiguo He (何治国)","url":"https://zhiguohe.net/data-and-empirical-patterns/intermediary-capital-ratio-and-risk-factor/"},{"title":"[PDF] Intermediary Asset Pricing: New Evidence from Many Asset Classes | Semantic Scholar","url":"https://www.semanticscholar.org/paper/Intermediary-Asset-Pricing:-New-Evidence-from-Many-He-Kelly/28428cea8e8e8fdc71ce549d6c5ca1193aace16b"},{"title":"The Role of Intermediaries in Asset Pricing | Becker Friedman Institute","url":"https://bfi.uchicago.edu/news/the-role-of-intermediaries-in-asset-pricing/"},{"title":"Annual Review of Financial Economics Intermediary Asset Pricing","url":"https://zhiguohe.net/wp-content/uploads/2023/12/annurev-financial-110217-022636.pdf"}]

I've completed a web search for research on the Federal Reserve intermediary capital ratio and asset pricing by He, Kelly, and Manela. Here are the key findings:

## Main Research

The primary research finds that shocks to the equity capital ratio of financial intermediaries—Primary Dealer counterparties of the New York Federal Reserve—possess significant explanatory power for cross-sectional variation in expected returns across equity and government bond market portfolios, as well as corporate and sovereign bonds, derivatives, commodities, and currencies.

The research examines shocks to the equity capital ratio of financial intermediaries—specifically Primary Dealer counterparties of the New York Federal Reserve—and their significant explanatory power for cross-sectional variation in expected returns.

## Key Findings

The intermediary capital risk factor is strongly pro-cyclical, implying counter-cyclical intermediary leverage. The price of risk for intermediary capital shocks is consistently positive and of similar magnitude when estimated separately for individual asset classes, suggesting that financial intermediaries are marginal investors in many markets.

## Publication and Data Updates

The original research was published in 2016-2017. Updated versions of the findings include quarterly data from 1970 to the present and daily data from 2023, with the time-series standardized to zero mean and unit variance.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about the Baker-Wurgler sentiment index and its role in stock return predictability:

## Overview of the Baker-Wurgler Index

The Baker and Wurgler Sentiment Index (BWSI) is a measure of investor sentiment that was developed by Malcolm Baker and Jeffrey Wurgler in 2006 through a paper called 'Investor Sentiment and the Cross-Section of Stock Returns'. The index attempts to quantify the degree of bullish or bearish sentiment in the stock market by analyzing the behavior of individual investors.

## Components and Construction

The sentiment index is a linear combination of six individual measures: Closed-end fund discount rate, share turnover, number of IPOs, first-day returns of IPOs, dividend premium and equity share in new issues.

## Predictability of Stock Returns

A high BWSI is associated with lower future stock market returns, while a low BWSI is associated with higher future stock market returns. However, the low forecasting power of that index for future aggregate stock market returns has long been a puzzle.

## Recent Research (2024-2025)

Recent studies have sought to improve upon the original index. An enhanced index represents an improved measure of investor sentiment: empirically, it not only instils forecasting power to the original Baker and Wurgler (2006) index, it also outperforms competitor measures in empirically capturing unobservable sentiment. Additionally, a new investor sentiment index was developed that confirms advantages in assessing the predictability towards stock returns compared to two common existing measures of investor sentiment: survey-based Consumer Sentiment Index and market-based Baker & Wurgler index.

results:
Based on the search results, here's what I found about the Baker-Wurgler sentiment index and its role in stock return predictability:

## Overview of the Baker-Wurgler Index

The Baker and Wurgler Sentiment Index (BWSI) is a measure of investor sentiment that was developed by Malcolm Baker and Jeffrey Wurgler in 2006 through a paper called 'Investor Sentiment and the Cross-Section of Stock Returns'. The index attempts to quantify the degree of bullish or bearish sentiment in the stock market by analyzing the behavior of individual investors.

## Components and Construction

The sentiment index is a linear combination of six individual measures: Closed-end fund discount rate, share turnover, number of IPOs, first-day returns of IPOs, dividend premium and equity share in new issues.

## Predictability of Stock Returns

A high BWSI is associated with lower future stock market returns, while a low BWSI is associated with higher future stock market returns. However, the low forecasting power of that index for future aggregate stock market returns has long been a puzzle.

## Recent Research (2024-2025)

Recent studies have sought to improve upon the original index. An enhanced index represents an improved measure of investor sentiment: empirically, it not only instils forecasting power to the original Baker and Wurgler (2006) index, it also outperforms competitor measures in empirically capturing unobservable sentiment. Additionally, a new investor sentiment index was developed that confirms advantages in assessing the predictability towards stock returns compared to two common existing measures of investor sentiment: survey-based Consumer Sentiment Index and market-based Baker & Wurgler index.

## Research Background

Foley, Karlsen, and Putniņš published research examining trafficking, darknet marketplace operation, and financial fraud. Their seminal work, "Sex, Drugs, and Bitcoin," found that around $76 billion of illegal activity per year involves bitcoin (46% of bitcoin transactions).

## 2024-2025 Illicit Activity Data

Recent data shows significant changes in crypto crime patterns:

**Overall Volume:** In 2024, crypto transaction volume grew to over USD 10.6 trillion, up 56% since 2023, while illicit volume dropped to USD 45 billion, down 24% since 2023, representing 0.4% of overall crypto volume.

**Crime Categories:** The top categories of illicit activity on the blockchain are Sanctions (33% of illicit volume), Blocklisted (29% of illicit volume), and Scams and Fraud (24% of illicit volume).

## Major Enforcement Events (2025)

On November 14, 2025, the Department of Justice announced five guilty pleas and over $15 million in civil forfeiture actions against the Democratic People's Republic of Korea for multimillion-dollar virtual currency heists at four overseas virtual currency platforms in 2023.

On October 14, 2025, the DOJ announced the largest ever forfeiture action against approximately $15 billion in Bitcoin tied to a multinational business conglomerate's alleged involvement in cryptocurrency investment fraud schemes.

I've performed the web search for your query about FX high frequency order flow, EBS, Reuters, intraday exchange rates, and 2024-2025 data. Here's what I found:

## Key Findings:

**EBS and Reuters as Leading FX Platforms:**
Electronic Broking Services (EBS) and Reuters Money (RM) are the leading interdealer platforms for FX trading, with EBS handling around 76 billion U.S. dollars in daily spot transaction volume.

**Recent EBS Market Developments (2024-2025):**
Recent research from CME Group shows significant activity on EBS in 2024-2025:
- In Q1 2024, EBS Market rolled out product enhancements including faster market data, lower minimum quote life, and reduced price increments to ¼ pips
- On November 6, 2024, following U.S. election results, spot USD/CNH on EBS traded an all-time record volume day of over $34 billion
- Looking forward to Q1 2025, EBS is launching FX Spot+, a revolutionary spot FX marketplace that connects futures liquidity with spot trading

**Intraday Exchange Rate Patterns:**
Exchange rate returns display predictable intraday seasonality, with the U.S. dollar appreciating in the run-up to FX fixes and depreciating thereafter, tracing a W-shaped return pattern around the clock.

**High-Frequency Order Flow Research:**
High-frequency FX datasets from EBS include trading volume in the global interdealer spot market, with volumes and prices at one-minute frequency, which supports research on order flow imbalances.

file.content:
---
globs: ["trade-rec-*.md", "report-*-trade-rec.html"]
---

# Risk Rules — Pre-Entry Checklist (binding)

Full rules in `framework/Risk Rules.md`. Summary for quick reference:

**Pre-entry — ALL must pass or no trade:**
1. |Sum| ≥ 3 with C scored (not blank)
2. Invalidation written, concrete, date-bounded
3. Correlation gate clean (shared regime variable = single theme, size to sector cap)
4. Per-position risk ≤ 2% AND post-entry portfolio heat ≤ 8%
5. ATR stop set (2–3× commodities/crypto, 1.5–2× equities)
6. Catalyst asymmetry stated (surprise vs confirmation dependent)

**Sizing:** Inverse-ATR with quarter-Kelly cap, max 25% single position.
**Heat limits:** 6–8% total, 25% sector cap, 5–15% crypto cap.
**Drawdown breakers:** −15% → 50% reduce; −20% → defensive (gold + cash).
**Correlation gate:** BTC + ETH ≈ one bet (~80% co-fire). Never double-count.

# Gerald's Trading Research System

Discretionary cross-asset + crypto trader using an evidence-graded 8-step methodology.
Timezone: UTC+8. "No trade" is always a valid output.

## Asset Universe (do not expand without permission)
Crypto: BTC, ETH. Equities: INTC, TSM, NVDA, TSLA, AAPL, GOOGL, AMZN, META, PYPL, PLTR, MU, WDC.
ETFs: QQQ, SPY, EWJ, EWY. Commodities: Brent, WTI, Gold, Silver, Copper, Palladium, Platinum.

# Evidence Grade Rules

When writing any trading analysis file:
- Cite evidence grades (A/B/C) on every variable and signal reference
- Grade A: replicated across samples, coherent economic mechanism, long history (momentum, carry, credit spreads, policy surprises, BTC order imbalance)
- Grade B: moderate, regime-dependent (news sentiment, options skew, MVRV/SOPR, exchange flows)
- Grade C: weak, narrative-heavy (stock-to-flow, halving cycles, seasonality)
- Never present Grade C as actionable without explicit qualification
- No stock-to-flow or halving-cycle timing — these are Grade C at best
- Fail-loud on MISSING Grade A: print `MISSING — [sources attempted]`, leave score leg blank

## computer-use
You have a computer-use MCP available (tools named `mcp__computer-use__*`). It lets you take screenshots of the user's desktop and control it with mouse clicks, keyboard input, and scrolling.

**Pick the right tool for the app.** Each tier trades speed/precision against coverage:

1. **Dedicated MCP for the app** — if the task is in an app that has its own MCP (Slack, Gmail, Calendar, Linear, etc.) and that MCP is connected, use it. API-backed tools are fast and precise.
2. **Chrome MCP** (`mcp__claude-in-chrome__*`) — if the target is a web app and there's no dedicated MCP for it, use the browser tools. DOM-aware, much faster than clicking pixels. If the Chrome extension isn't connected, ask the user to install it rather than falling through to computer use.
3. **Computer use** — for native desktop apps (Maps, Notes, Finder, Photos, System Settings, any third-party native app) and cross-app workflows. Computer use IS the right tool here — don't decline a native-app task just because there's no dedicated MCP for it.

This is about what's available, not error handling — if a dedicated MCP tool errors, debug or report it rather than silently retrying via a slower tier.

**Look before you assert.** If the user asks about app state (what's open, what's connected, what an app can do), take a screenshot and check before answering. Don't answer from memory — the user's setup or app version may differ from what you expect. If you're about to say an app doesn't support an action, that claim should be grounded in what you just saw on screen, not general knowledge. Similarly, `list_granted_applications` or a fresh `screenshot` is cheaper than a wrong assertion about what's running.

**Loading via ToolSearch — load in bulk, not one-by-one:** if computer-use tools are in the deferred list, load them ALL in a single ToolSearch call: `{ query: "computer-use", max_results: 30 }`. The keyword search matches the server-name substring in every tool name, so one query returns the entire toolkit. Don't use `select:` for individual tools — that's one round-trip per tool.

**Access flow:** before any computer-use action you must call `request_access` with the list of applications you need. The user approves each application explicitly, and you may need to call it again mid-task if you discover you need another application.

**Tiered apps:** some apps are granted at a restricted tier based on their category — the tier is displayed in the approval dialog and returned in the `request_access` response:
- **Browsers** (Safari, Chrome, Firefox, Edge, Arc, etc.) → tier **"read"**: visible in screenshots, but clicks and typing are blocked. You can read what's already on screen. For navigation, clicking, or form-filling, use the claude-in-chrome MCP (tools named `mcp__claude-in-chrome__*`; load via ToolSearch if deferred).
- **Terminals and IDEs** (Terminal, iTerm, VS Code, JetBrains, etc.) → tier **"click"**: visible and left-clickable, but typing, key presses, right-click, modifier-clicks, and drag-drop are blocked. You can click a Run button or scroll test output, but cannot type into the editor or integrated terminal, cannot right-click (the context menu has Paste), and cannot drag text onto them. For shell commands, use the Bash tool.
- **Everything else** → tier **"full"**: no restrictions.

The tier is enforced by the frontmost-app check: if a tier-"read" app is in front, `left_click` returns an error; if a tier-"click" app is in front, `type` and `right_click` return errors. The error tells you what tier the app has and what to do instead. `open_application` works at any tier — bringing an app forward is a read-level operation.

**Link safety — treat links in emails and messages as suspicious by default.**
- **Never click web links with computer-use tools.** If you encounter a link in a native app (Mail, Messages, a PDF, etc.), do NOT `left_click` it. Open the URL via the claude-in-chrome MCP instead.
- **See the full URL before following any link.** Visible link text can be misleading — hover or inspect to get the real destination.
- **Links from emails, messages, or unknown-sender documents are suspicious by default.** If the destination URL is at all unfamiliar or looks off, ask the user for confirmation before proceeding.
- **Inside the Chrome extension** you can click links with the extension's tools, but the suspicion check still applies — verify unfamiliar URLs with the user.

**Financial actions - do not execute trades or move money.** Budgeting and accounting apps (Quicken, YNAB, QuickBooks, etc.) are granted at full tier so you can categorize transactions, generate reports, and help the user organize their finances. But never execute a trade, place an order, send money, or initiate a transfer on the user's behalf - always ask the user to perform those actions themselves.

content.content:
285	## 5. Literature engagement — the eight strands
286	
287	### 5.1 Intraday momentum (Gao-Han-Li-Zhou 2018 RFS; Zhang-Wang 2022 JBF; Zarattini-Aziz-Barbon 2024 SSRN #4824172) — Grade A
288	
289	The first-half-hour return predicts the last-half-hour return at t > 4 in US equities (GHLZ 2018), replicated OOS internationally (Zhang-Wang 2022). Zarattini-Aziz-Barbon (2024) report Sharpe 1.33 on SPY intraday-momentum strategy from 2007–2024. **Engagement:** this is a *separate* signal at a separate frequency, not an uplift to existing 12m TSMOM. Out of scope per §1 (no new variables). **Also note:** 5×/day ≈ 80-min spacing is *too coarse* to capture the GHLZ first-half/last-half-hour signal; 5×/day would collapse open and close half-hours into single bars each. **Implication:** this strand does not change any existing variable's ranking. Flag for `quarterly-methodology-review` as a potential *new* variable — but out of scope for this memo. (Also: 2024–2025 reports of signal decay in intraday momentum — Zarattini et al. note "well-studied factors such as overnight effects and intraday momentum have diminished over time" — so decay risk is elevated vs the 2018 baseline.)
290	
291	### 5.2 Realized-volatility microstructure (ABDL 2001 JASA; Bollerslev-Patton-Quaedvlieg 2016 JoE; HF-SV models 2024 ScienceDirect) — Grade A
292	
293	RV variance falls ~1/N sampling rate **up to the microstructure-noise floor** (5-min for liquid US equities; finer for crypto). 5×/day is at the *very coarse* end — far below the noise floor — so uplift is monotonically positive for every RV variable. 2024 HF stochastic-vol work on E-mini S&P 500 futures confirms HAR models improve with higher-frequency data. **Direct cite for:** R01–R05 (all RV rows, IUS 3 across single-stock / index / commodity / FX / crypto), R16–R20 (VRP, IUS 2), R21 (cross-asset realized correlation, IUS 2). **Strongest single justification for the top of Ranking B and Ranking B-ES.**
294	
295	### 5.3 VPIN / order-flow toxicity (Easley-López de Prado-O'Hara 2012 RFS; Abad-Yagüe 2018 JIFMIM; arxiv 2508.06788 2025 OFI S&P 500 E-mini) — Grade A
296	
297	VPIN computes flow imbalance over volume buckets; shown to lead the May 2010 Flash Crash by ~30 min. The signal is *defined* on intraday volume — EOD cannot reproduce it. 2025 arxiv work on S&P 500 E-mini at 1-second frequency reinforces the OFI→return link and shows macro-news announcements reshape the dynamic. **Engagement:** canonical VPIN uses 50-bucket aggregations within the day; 5×/day captures only ~5 reads, so direction preserved with reduced power. **Direct cite for:** T10 (VPIN single-stock, IUS 3), T11 (VPIN index, IUS 3), T12 (VPIN commodity, IUS 2), K01 (BTC/ETH order imbalance, IUS 3). Med-whipsaw flag reflects 5×/day coarseness vs canonical 50-bucket VPIN.
298	
299	### 5.4 Options-implied information (Cremers-Weinbaum 2010 JFQA; An-Ang-Bali-Cakici 2014 JF; MDPI FRM 2024 17/1/39) — Grade A
300	
301	Put-call IV spreads predict next-day stock returns at t > 4, decay in 1–2 days (CW 2010). An et al. (2014) jointly characterize stock-option return predictability. **2024 MDPI study is the most recent single-stock-specific evidence:** implements intraday model-free IV at 1m/10m/60m windows and directly validates the return-vol relationship at those frequencies. **⚠ Single-asset-evidence note:** CW 2010 and An et al. 2014 are both US single-stock only — the mechanism is argued to generalize to index/commodity/crypto/FX skew but IUS is capped at 2 for those extrapolated rows (R12–R15). **Direct cite for:** R11 (single-stock skew, IUS 3), R08 (single-name IV, IUS 3), R12–R15 (skew extrapolations, IUS 2, all ⚠).
302	
303	### 5.5 ETF flow intraday signal (Ben-David-Franzoni-Moussawi 2018 JF; Brown-Davies-Ringgenberg 2021 JFE; Lazo-Paz 2024 AEA; 2024–25 BTC-ETF flow papers) — Grade A equity, Grade B crypto
304	
305	BFM 2018 documents ETF arbitrage producing intraday return-predictability via creation/redemption observable through premium/discount + AP volume. Lazo-Paz (2024 AEA) builds an ETF-based stock-price-fragility measure. Creation predicts next-day positive returns **conditional on a premium** (not symmetric). **Engagement:** flow is intraday-inferable; canonical NAV is daily. 5×/day proxy captures AP-arb signal EOD reports miss. Med-cost flag reflects difficulty of building the proxy (premium/discount + AP volume) from public data. **Direct cite for:** R33 (equity ETF flow, IUS 2), R34 (BTC/ETH ETF flow, IUS 2).
306	
307	### 5.6 On-chain data frequency (Makarov-Schoar 2020 JFE; Liu-Tsyvinski 2021 RFS; Cong-Li-Wang 2021 RFS; Pagnotta 2022 RFS) — Grade A on variables, Grade B on intraday-specific evidence
308	
309	The crypto asset-pricing literature uses **daily** on-chain features (active addresses, exchange flows, hash rate). Liu-Tsyvinski's network-effect factor is a daily statistic. **Engagement:** there is **NO Grade-A evidence** for intraday on-chain data adding alpha vs daily aggregates *for the network-effect family* (active addresses, hash rate). For exchange netflows and stablecoin issuance, large-event detection at sub-daily frequency has Grade B support — large outflows cluster in hours. 2025 order-flow crypto work (EFMA, arxiv 2506.05764, PMC10040314) reinforces that **order-imbalance and flow-based** variables have native intraday information, while **state variables** (address counts, hash rate) retain their daily canonical horizon. **Direct cite for:** K01 (order imbalance, IUS 3), K02 (active addresses, IUS 1 — diurnal cycle noise), K03 (hash rate, IUS 1), R39 (stablecoin, IUS 2), R40 (exchange netflows, IUS 2), K04 (MVRV/SOPR, IUS 0 — daily compute).
310	
311	### 5.7 Dealer gamma / GEX intraday (Barbon-Buraschi 2021 WP; Ni-Pearson-Poteshman 2005 RFS; practitioner GEX literature 2024–25) — Grade B (single-paper for V030)
312	
313	GEX is a real-time function of options OI weighted by distance from strike — refreshes whenever options trade. Barbon-Buraschi show short-gamma regimes amplify intraday vol; regime can flip within a session. 2024–25 practitioner sources (GEXBoard, SpotGamma, Glassnode) document 0DTE hedging windows at open/mid-morning/close — mechanistically consistent but Grade C on their own. **⚠ Engagement:** strong intraday-uplift *mechanism*, but V030 evidence base is single academic paper (per Methodology §4 V030 entry) and canonical data source (SqueezeMetrics) is paid. **Direct cite for:** R36 / R37 / R38 (V030 DealerGamma, IUS 3 raw on mechanism; net score 1.5 due to High cost — knocks out of Ranking B-ES top-5 despite perfect IUS). Evidence grade stays B until second replication.
314	
315	### 5.8 CFTC / positioning (weekly Tuesday release) — explicit no-uplift case
316	
317	**Engagement:** Friday-close-as-of, Tuesday-released. No intraday source exists. 5×/day returns the same value 5 times per day. **Direct cite for:** T08/T09 (CFTC speculative positioning, IUS 0), R29 (crowding/positioning CFTC component, IUS 0). This is the canonical example of why the §3 "does 5×/day actually change the observed value" gatekeeper matters — without it, an enthusiastic upsample plan wastes capacity on 5 daily reads of an unchanging weekly value. **Same logic applies to R35 V027 Intermediary capital** (weekly NY Fed release) — confirmed by this week's signal-review log where V027 showed 0 contribution.
318	
319	---
320	
321	## 6. Decision-grade recommendation
322	
323	### 6.1 Top 5 from Ranking B-ES (index + single-stock) — **the primary action list**
324	
325	Gerald's book sits on single-stock + QQQ/SPY/EWJ/EWY. Act from these first.
326	
327	1. **R01 / R02 Realized vol & ATR for single-stock and index (score 3.0 each)** — directly addresses this week's EWY-stop lesson from `signal-review-2026-04-20.md` (stop tightened intraday on a noise wick). 5×/day RV-based ATR re-estimate would have given the methodology stop a wider vol-aware band, preventing premature exit. **P&L channel:** fewer noise-wick exits during the hold window, especially on 1.5–2× ATR equity stops. The EWY-style execution loss is the binding weakness per §2 gap framing.
328	
329	2. **R06 VIX at 5×/day (score 3.0)** — the R-overlay input that determines "downgrade R from +1 to 0" and "widen stop one notch" decisions for the equity sleeve. EOD forces same-day decisions on yesterday's regime; 5×/day captures intraday regime flips (midday VIX spike, end-of-session crush) that would otherwise be acted on next session. **P&L channel:** better timing of R-overlay notches on |Sum|≥3 equity entries; faster recognition of crowding/stress before EOD print.
330	
331	3. **R08 Single-name IV for 12 tickers (score 3.0)** — analog of VIX at single-name level. 2024 MDPI evidence directly validates single-stock intraday IV (1m/10m/60m) for return-vol relationship. Supports single-stock R-leg on the 12-ticker sleeve where Gerald's structural bets (V026/V029/V031/V032) live. **P&L channel:** single-stock R-overlay responsiveness during earnings run-ups and catalyst weeks, where single-name IV moves independently of VIX.
332	
333	4. **R11 Equity skew / risk-reversals — single-stock (score 3.0, ⚠ single-asset evidence)** — Cremers-Weinbaum 2010 is the native evidence class; 2024 MDPI confirms at intraday frequency. Skew flip within a session is a leading indicator of next-day directional move; EOD skew loses this. ⚠ caveat: evidence is US single-stock only, but that *is* Gerald's universe here. **P&L channel:** tighter tactical-leg (T) entry timing on single-stock |Sum|≥3 signals where options market leads cash; supplements V026 residual momentum.
334	
335	5. **R24 / R25 Liquidity (spreads, depth) — single-stock and equity-index (score 3.0 each)** — real-time at source. 5×/day samples the day's liquidity profile (open, midday, close). Matters for fill quality and stop placement — particularly for single-stock entries around earnings (thin liquidity + wide spread is a pre-entry-checklist violation per Risk Rules §1). **P&L channel:** fewer high-slippage entries/exits; more accurate per-position risk sizing when ATR stop meets realized spread.
336	
337	(Ranks 6–8 worth noting — knocked out of Top-5 by bucket tiebreak: **R17 VRP single-stock (2.0), T10 VPIN single-stock (2.0), C01 earnings catalyst single-stock (2.5)**. C01 earns a specific note: 5×/day uplift is concentrated on T-0/T+1 of scheduled earnings — non-event days see zero uplift. Implement by turning on the higher cadence only during the earnings week for each ticker.)
338	
339	### 6.2 Top 3 from Ranking B (all assets, outside the equity sleeve) — cross-asset cross-check
340	
341	1. **R05 BTC/ETH realized vol + jumps (score 3.0)** + **K01 BTC/ETH order imbalance (score 2.5)** — crypto trades 24/7, so EOD is an arbitrary cut; *any* intraday refresh is closer to the data-generating process. Order imbalance is the highest-graded BTC predictor in Coin core. 2024–25 crypto microstructure work (arxiv 2506.05764, EFMA 2025 order-flow) reinforces OOS predictive power. **P&L channel:** faster entry/exit timing on BTC/ETH theses where Sum tips on imbalance + vol regime; reduces lag vs proprietary crypto desks.
342	
343	2. **R03 Realized vol / ATR — commodity (score 3.0)** — 2024 WTI HAR-ML (Luo JoF) and 2023 gold intraday RV (Xia FinLetters) provide direct commodity-specific evidence. Commodity ATR stops are typically 2–3× vs 1.5–2× for equities (Risk Rules), so RV precision matters more for sizing. **P&L channel:** better stop placement on commodity longs (Gold, Brent, Copper) — directly relevant to the open Gold +3 thesis (N029).
344	
345	3. **R22 Credit spreads (CDX HY as HY OAS proxy) (score 2.0)** — CDX HY trades intraday, EOD HY OAS index is lagged. 1–2 hr lead vs EOD print on HY OAS regime — relevant for V004 HY OAS R-leg when regime is shifting. Low cost, low whipsaw. **P&L channel:** earlier detection of HY-OAS widening stress, tightening cross-asset R-overlay before EOD print confirms.
346	
347	### 6.3 Top 3 to explicitly NOT upsample
348	
349	1. **V033/V034/V035 Faber TAA (Overlay, IUS 0 — forbidden)** — rule is month-end close vs 10m-SMA. 5×/day generates within-month sleeve flips the Faber circuit-breaker is *designed* to prevent. Upsampling would *degrade* this variable. Methodology §1.5 explicitly says "do not recompute intraday."
350	
351	2. **V031 GP/A and V032 CEI (S, IUS 0)** — quarterly-statement-bound. Binding constraint is reporting lag, not sampling frequency. 5×/day = identical value 5 times daily and creates a false impression of new information. Same logic applies to V009/V026 factor residualization (FF5 factors update monthly).
352	
353	3. **CFTC speculative positioning (T, IUS 0) and V027 Intermediary capital (R, IUS 0)** — both weekly-release-bound. 5×/day = identical value 5 times. Signal-review log shows V027 = 0 contribution this regime — that's a regime feature, not a sampling problem. Upsampling does not fix it.
354	
355	### 6.4 Data-feed implications
356	
357	Top-5 B-ES implicates only already-low-cost equity feeds:
358	- **R01/R02 RV/ATR + R24/R25 liquidity + R06 VIX:** intraday US equity + futures tape (already available via polygon.io / IBKR), CBOE intraday VIX (Bloomberg/CBOE feed — Low cost).
359	- **R08 single-name IV + R11 equity skew:** equity options surface — requires CBOE DataShop or polygon.io options feed (Med cost if not already provisioned). This is the only incremental spend for the equity-sleeve top-5.
360	
361	Cross-asset Top-3 adds: **R05/K01 BTC/ETH intraday** — free via Binance/Bybit/Coinbase public APIs + Deribit public for IV/skew. **R03 commodity RV** — futures tape (already available). **R22 CDX HY** — Markit/ICE (Med cost, optional for proxy).
362	
363	**No SqueezeMetrics GEX subscription required** — V030 (R36/R37/R38) sits at rank 33+ in Ranking B precisely because High cost doesn't justify it ahead of cheaper R-overlay wins. Revisit if a second GEX paper reaches peer-review (currently single-paper Grade B per §4 V030).
364	
365	### 6.5 Six-month review gate (matches 2026-10-14 audit-addition cohort)
366	
367	**Single metric:** exit-quality delta on signals where the affected variable was in the R-overlay leg or stop-sizing input, comparing 6 months pre-upsample vs 6 months post-upsample.
368	
369	- **Exit-quality delta.** For each closed trade in the post-upsample window, compute `(actual exit P&L) − (P&L at methodology-stop exit)`. If 5×/day RV/ATR genuinely helps, this delta should improve by ≥0.3R on average (matching EWY-style execution losses).
370	- **Threshold:** ≥0.2R improvement on closed trades, sample ≥10, otherwise demote the upsample to "no decision-moving contribution" and revert that variable to EOD.
371	- Pair with the existing 2026-10-14 audit-addition review for residual-momentum, intermediary-capital, and basis-momentum so all 2026-Q2 methodology changes review in one session.
372	
373	---

378	- All ⚠ rows explicitly acknowledged in the single-asset-evidence column per preamble rule; IUS capped at 2 for extrapolation rows (R12/R13/R15, R38).
379	- `INSUFFICIENT EVIDENCE` rows (S05, S12, S14) scored IUS 0, not 1.

## 5. Literature engagement — the eight strands

### 5.1 Intraday momentum (Gao-Han-Li-Zhou 2018 RFS; Zhang-Wang 2022 JBF; Zarattini-Aziz-Barbon 2024 SSRN #4824172) — Grade A

The first-half-hour return predicts the last-half-hour return at t > 4 in US equities (GHLZ 2018), replicated OOS internationally (Zhang-Wang 2022). Zarattini-Aziz-Barbon (2024) report Sharpe 1.33 on SPY intraday-momentum strategy from 2007–2024. **Engagement:** this is a *separate* signal at a separate frequency, not an uplift to existing 12m TSMOM. Out of scope per §1 (no new variables). **Also note:** 5×/day ≈ 80-min spacing is *too coarse* to capture the GHLZ first-half/last-half-hour signal; 5×/day would collapse open and close half-hours into single bars each. **Implication:** this strand does not change any existing variable's ranking. Flag for `quarterly-methodology-review` as a potential *new* variable — but out of scope for this memo. (Also: 2024–2025 reports of signal decay in intraday momentum — Zarattini et al. note "well-studied factors such as overnight effects and intraday momentum have diminished over time" — so decay risk is elevated vs the 2018 baseline.)

### 5.2 Realized-volatility microstructure (ABDL 2001 JASA; Bollerslev-Patton-Quaedvlieg 2016 JoE; HF-SV models 2024 ScienceDirect) — Grade A

RV variance falls ~1/N sampling rate **up to the microstructure-noise floor** (5-min for liquid US equities; finer for crypto). 5×/day is at the *very coarse* end — far below the noise floor — so uplift is monotonically positive for every RV variable. 2024 HF stochastic-vol work on E-mini S&P 500 futures confirms HAR models improve with higher-frequency data. **Direct cite for:** R01–R05 (all RV rows, IUS 3 across single-stock / index / commodity / FX / crypto), R16–R20 (VRP, IUS 2), R21 (cross-asset realized correlation, IUS 2). **Strongest single justification for the top of Ranking B and Ranking B-ES.**

### 5.3 VPIN / order-flow toxicity (Easley-López de Prado-O'Hara 2012 RFS; Abad-Yagüe 2018 JIFMIM; arxiv 2508.06788 2025 OFI S&P 500 E-mini) — Grade A

VPIN computes flow imbalance over volume buckets; shown to lead the May 2010 Flash Crash by ~30 min. The signal is *defined* on intraday volume — EOD cannot reproduce it. 2025 arxiv work on S&P 500 E-mini at 1-second frequency reinforces the OFI→return link and shows macro-news announcements reshape the dynamic. **Engagement:** canonical VPIN uses 50-bucket aggregations within the day; 5×/day captures only ~5 reads, so direction preserved with reduced power. **Direct cite for:** T10 (VPIN single-stock, IUS 3), T11 (VPIN index, IUS 3), T12 (VPIN commodity, IUS 2), K01 (BTC/ETH order imbalance, IUS 3). Med-whipsaw flag reflects 5×/day coarseness vs canonical 50-bucket VPIN.

### 5.4 Options-implied information (Cremers-Weinbaum 2010 JFQA; An-Ang-Bali-Cakici 2014 JF; MDPI FRM 2024 17/1/39) — Grade A

Put-call IV spreads predict next-day stock returns at t > 4, decay in 1–2 days (CW 2010). An et al. (2014) jointly characterize stock-option return predictability. **2024 MDPI study is the most recent single-stock-specific evidence:** implements intraday model-free IV at 1m/10m/60m windows and directly validates the return-vol relationship at those frequencies. **⚠ Single-asset-evidence note:** CW 2010 and An et al. 2014 are both US single-stock only — the mechanism is argued to generalize to index/commodity/crypto/FX skew but IUS is capped at 2 for those extrapolated rows (R12–R15). **Direct cite for:** R11 (single-stock skew, IUS 3), R08 (single-name IV, IUS 3), R12–R15 (skew extrapolations, IUS 2, all ⚠).

### 5.5 ETF flow intraday signal (Ben-David-Franzoni-Moussawi 2018 JF; Brown-Davies-Ringgenberg 2021 JFE; Lazo-Paz 2024 AEA; 2024–25 BTC-ETF flow papers) — Grade A equity, Grade B crypto

BFM 2018 documents ETF arbitrage producing intraday return-predictability via creation/redemption observable through premium/discount + AP volume. Lazo-Paz (2024 AEA) builds an ETF-based stock-price-fragility measure. Creation predicts next-day positive returns **conditional on a premium** (not symmetric). **Engagement:** flow is intraday-inferable; canonical NAV is daily. 5×/day proxy captures AP-arb signal EOD reports miss. Med-cost flag reflects difficulty of building the proxy (premium/discount + AP volume) from public data. **Direct cite for:** R33 (equity ETF flow, IUS 2), R34 (BTC/ETH ETF flow, IUS 2).

### 5.6 On-chain data frequency (Makarov-Schoar 2020 JFE; Liu-Tsyvinski 2021 RFS; Cong-Li-Wang 2021 RFS; Pagnotta 2022 RFS) — Grade A on variables, Grade B on intraday-specific evidence

The crypto asset-pricing literature uses **daily** on-chain features (active addresses, exchange flows, hash rate). Liu-Tsyvinski's network-effect factor is a daily statistic. **Engagement:** there is **NO Grade-A evidence** for intraday on-chain data adding alpha vs daily aggregates *for the network-effect family* (active addresses, hash rate). For exchange netflows and stablecoin issuance, large-event detection at sub-daily frequency has Grade B support — large outflows cluster in hours. 2025 order-flow crypto work (EFMA, arxiv 2506.05764, PMC10040314) reinforces that **order-imbalance and flow-based** variables have native intraday information, while **state variables** (address counts, hash rate) retain their daily canonical horizon. **Direct cite for:** K01 (order imbalance, IUS 3), K02 (active addresses, IUS 1 — diurnal cycle noise), K03 (hash rate, IUS 1), R39 (stablecoin, IUS 2), R40 (exchange netflows, IUS 2), K04 (MVRV/SOPR, IUS 0 — daily compute).

### 5.7 Dealer gamma / GEX intraday (Barbon-Buraschi 2021 WP; Ni-Pearson-Poteshman 2005 RFS; practitioner GEX literature 2024–25) — Grade B (single-paper for V030)

GEX is a real-time function of options OI weighted by distance from strike — refreshes whenever options trade. Barbon-Buraschi show short-gamma regimes amplify intraday vol; regime can flip within a session. 2024–25 practitioner sources (GEXBoard, SpotGamma, Glassnode) document 0DTE hedging windows at open/mid-morning/close — mechanistically consistent but Grade C on their own. **⚠ Engagement:** strong intraday-uplift *mechanism*, but V030 evidence base is single academic paper (per Methodology §4 V030 entry) and canonical data source (SqueezeMetrics) is paid. **Direct cite for:** R36 / R37 / R38 (V030 DealerGamma, IUS 3 raw on mechanism; net score 1.5 due to High cost — knocks out of Ranking B-ES top-5 despite perfect IUS). Evidence grade stays B until second replication.

### 5.8 CFTC / positioning (weekly Tuesday release) — explicit no-uplift case

**Engagement:** Friday-close-as-of, Tuesday-released. No intraday source exists. 5×/day returns the same value 5 times per day. **Direct cite for:** T08/T09 (CFTC speculative positioning, IUS 0), R29 (crowding/positioning CFTC component, IUS 0). This is the canonical example of why the §3 "does 5×/day actually change the observed value" gatekeeper matters — without it, an enthusiastic upsample plan wastes capacity on 5 daily reads of an unchanging weekly value. **Same logic applies to R35 V027 Intermediary capital** (weekly NY Fed release) — confirmed by this week's signal-review log where V027 showed 0 contribution.

---

## 6. Decision-grade recommendation

### 6.1 Top 5 from Ranking B-ES (index + single-stock) — **the primary action list**

Gerald's book sits on single-stock + QQQ/SPY/EWJ/EWY. Act from these first.

1. **R01 / R02 Realized vol & ATR for single-stock and index (score 3.0 each)** — directly addresses this week's EWY-stop lesson from `signal-review-2026-04-20.md` (stop tightened intraday on a noise wick). 5×/day RV-based ATR re-estimate would have given the methodology stop a wider vol-aware band, preventing premature exit. **P&L channel:** fewer noise-wick exits during the hold window, especially on 1.5–2× ATR equity stops. The EWY-style execution loss is the binding weakness per §2 gap framing.

2. **R06 VIX at 5×/day (score 3.0)** — the R-overlay input that determines "downgrade R from +1 to 0" and "widen stop one notch" decisions for the equity sleeve. EOD forces same-day decisions on yesterday's regime; 5×/day captures intraday regime flips (midday VIX spike, end-of-session crush) that would otherwise be acted on next session. **P&L channel:** better timing of R-overlay notches on |Sum|≥3 equity entries; faster recognition of crowding/stress before EOD print.

3. **R08 Single-name IV for 12 tickers (score 3.0)** — analog of VIX at single-name level. 2024 MDPI evidence directly validates single-stock intraday IV (1m/10m/60m) for return-vol relationship. Supports single-stock R-leg on the 12-ticker sleeve where Gerald's structural bets (V026/V029/V031/V032) live. **P&L channel:** single-stock R-overlay responsiveness during earnings run-ups and catalyst weeks, where single-name IV moves independently of VIX.

4. **R11 Equity skew / risk-reversals — single-stock (score 3.0, ⚠ single-asset evidence)** — Cremers-Weinbaum 2010 is the native evidence class; 2024 MDPI confirms at intraday frequency. Skew flip within a session is a leading indicator of next-day directional move; EOD skew loses this. ⚠ caveat: evidence is US single-stock only, but that *is* Gerald's universe here. **P&L channel:** tighter tactical-leg (T) entry timing on single-stock |Sum|≥3 signals where options market leads cash; supplements V026 residual momentum.

5. **R24 / R25 Liquidity (spreads, depth) — single-stock and equity-index (score 3.0 each)** — real-time at source. 5×/day samples the day's liquidity profile (open, midday, close). Matters for fill quality and stop placement — particularly for single-stock entries around earnings (thin liquidity + wide spread is a pre-entry-checklist violation per Risk Rules §1). **P&L channel:** fewer high-slippage entries/exits; more accurate per-position risk sizing when ATR stop meets realized spread.

(Ranks 6–8 worth noting — knocked out of Top-5 by bucket tiebreak: **R17 VRP single-stock (2.0), T10 VPIN single-stock (2.0), C01 earnings catalyst single-stock (2.5)**. C01 earns a specific note: 5×/day uplift is concentrated on T-0/T+1 of scheduled earnings — non-event days see zero uplift. Implement by turning on the higher cadence only during the earnings week for each ticker.)

### 6.2 Top 3 from Ranking B (all assets, outside the equity sleeve) — cross-asset cross-check

1. **R05 BTC/ETH realized vol + jumps (score 3.0)** + **K01 BTC/ETH order imbalance (score 2.5)** — crypto trades 24/7, so EOD is an arbitrary cut; *any* intraday refresh is closer to the data-generating process. Order imbalance is the highest-graded BTC predictor in Coin core. 2024–25 crypto microstructure work (arxiv 2506.05764, EFMA 2025 order-flow) reinforces OOS predictive power. **P&L channel:** faster entry/exit timing on BTC/ETH theses where Sum tips on imbalance + vol regime; reduces lag vs proprietary crypto desks.

2. **R03 Realized vol / ATR — commodity (score 3.0)** — 2024 WTI HAR-ML (Luo JoF) and 2023 gold intraday RV (Xia FinLetters) provide direct commodity-specific evidence. Commodity ATR stops are typically 2–3× vs 1.5–2× for equities (Risk Rules), so RV precision matters more for sizing. **P&L channel:** better stop placement on commodity longs (Gold, Brent, Copper) — directly relevant to the open Gold +3 thesis (N029).

3. **R22 Credit spreads (CDX HY as HY OAS proxy) (score 2.0)** — CDX HY trades intraday, EOD HY OAS index is lagged. 1–2 hr lead vs EOD print on HY OAS regime — relevant for V004 HY OAS R-leg when regime is shifting. Low cost, low whipsaw. **P&L channel:** earlier detection of HY-OAS widening stress, tightening cross-asset R-overlay before EOD print confirms.

### 6.3 Top 3 to explicitly NOT upsample

1. **V033/V034/V035 Faber TAA (Overlay, IUS 0 — forbidden)** — rule is month-end close vs 10m-SMA. 5×/day generates within-month sleeve flips the Faber circuit-breaker is *designed* to prevent. Upsampling would *degrade* this variable. Methodology §1.5 explicitly says "do not recompute intraday."

2. **V031 GP/A and V032 CEI (S, IUS 0)** — quarterly-statement-bound. Binding constraint is reporting lag, not sampling frequency. 5×/day = identical value 5 times daily and creates a false impression of new information. Same logic applies to V009/V026 factor residualization (FF5 factors update monthly).

3. **CFTC speculative positioning (T, IUS 0) and V027 Intermediary capital (R, IUS 0)** — both weekly-release-bound. 5×/day = identical value 5 times. Signal-review log shows V027 = 0 contribution this regime — that's a regime feature, not a sampling problem. Upsampling does not fix it.

### 6.4 Data-feed implications

Top-5 B-ES implicates only already-low-cost equity feeds:
- **R01/R02 RV/ATR + R24/R25 liquidity + R06 VIX:** intraday US equity + futures tape (already available via polygon.io / IBKR), CBOE intraday VIX (Bloomberg/CBOE feed — Low cost).
- **R08 single-name IV + R11 equity skew:** equity options surface — requires CBOE DataShop or polygon.io options feed (Med cost if not already provisioned). This is the only incremental spend for the equity-sleeve top-5.

Cross-asset Top-3 adds: **R05/K01 BTC/ETH intraday** — free via Binance/Bybit/Coinbase public APIs + Deribit public for IV/skew. **R03 commodity RV** — futures tape (already available). **R22 CDX HY** — Markit/ICE (Med cost, optional for proxy).

**No SqueezeMetrics GEX subscription required** — V030 (R36/R37/R38) sits at rank 33+ in Ranking B precisely because High cost doesn't justify it ahead of cheaper R-overlay wins. Revisit if a second GEX paper reaches peer-review (currently single-paper Grade B per §4 V030).

### 6.5 Six-month review gate (matches 2026-10-14 audit-addition cohort)

**Single metric:** exit-quality delta on signals where the affected variable was in the R-overlay leg or stop-sizing input, comparing 6 months pre-upsample vs 6 months post-upsample.

- **Exit-quality delta.** For each closed trade in the post-upsample window, compute `(actual exit P&L) − (P&L at methodology-stop exit)`. If 5×/day RV/ATR genuinely helps, this delta should improve by ≥0.3R on average (matching EWY-style execution losses).
- **Threshold:** ≥0.2R improvement on closed trades, sample ≥10, otherwise demote the upsample to "no decision-moving contribution" and revert that variable to EOD.
- Pair with the existing 2026-10-14 audit-addition review for residual-momentum, intermediary-capital, and basis-momentum so all 2026-Q2 methodology changes review in one session.

input.old_string:
### 5.1 Intraday momentum (Gao-Han-Li-Zhou 2018 RFS; Zhang-Wang 2022 JBF; Zarattini-Aziz-Barbon 2024 SSRN #4824172) — Grade A

The first-half-hour return predicts the last-half-hour return at t > 4 in US equities (GHLZ 2018), replicated OOS internationally (Zhang-Wang 2022). Zarattini-Aziz-Barbon (2024) report Sharpe 1.33 on SPY intraday-momentum strategy from 2007–2024. **Engagement:** this is a *separate* signal at a separate frequency, not an uplift to existing 12m TSMOM. Out of scope per §1 (no new variables). **Also note:** 5×/day ≈ 80-min spacing is *too coarse* to capture the GHLZ first-half/last-half-hour signal; 5×/day would collapse open and close half-hours into single bars each. **Implication:** this strand does not change any existing variable's ranking. Flag for `quarterly-methodology-review` as a potential *new* variable — but out of scope for this memo. (Also: 2024–2025 reports of signal decay in intraday momentum — Zarattini et al. note "well-studied factors such as overnight effects and intraday momentum have diminished over time" — so decay risk is elevated vs the 2018 baseline.)

Out of scope per §1 (no new variables). **Also note:** 5×/day ≈ 80-min spacing is *too coarse* to capture the GHLZ first-half/last-half-hour signal; 5×/day would collapse open and close half-hours into single bars each. **Implication:** this strand does not change any existing variable's ranking. Flag for `quarterly-methodology-review` as a potential *new* variable — but out of scope for this memo. (Also: 2024–2025 reports of signal decay in intraday momentum — Zarattini et al. note "well-studied factors such as overnight effects and intraday momentum have diminished over time" — so decay risk is elevated vs the 2018 baseline.)

### 5.2 Realized-volatility microstructure (ABDL 2001 JASA; Bollerslev-Patton-Quaedvlieg 2016 JoE; HF-SV models 2024 ScienceDirect) — Grade A

input.old_string:
### 5.2 Realized-volatility microstructure (ABDL 2001 JASA; Bollerslev-Patton-Quaedvlieg 2016 JoE; HF-SV models 2024 ScienceDirect) — Grade A

RV variance falls ~1/N sampling rate **up to the microstructure-noise floor** (5-min for liquid US equities; finer for crypto). 5×/day is at the *very coarse* end — far below the noise floor — so uplift is monotonically positive for every RV variable. 2024 HF stochastic-vol work on E-mini S&P 500 futures confirms HAR models improve with higher-frequency data. **Direct cite for:** R01–R05 (all RV rows, IUS 3 across single-stock / index / commodity / FX / crypto), R16–R20 (VRP, IUS 2), R21 (cross-asset realized correlation, IUS 2). **Strongest single justification for the top of Ranking B and Ranking B-ES.**
input.new_string:
### 5.2 Realized-volatility microstructure (ABDL 2001 JASA; Bollerslev-Patton-Quaedvlieg 2016 JoE; HF-SV models 2024 ScienceDirect) — Grade A

RV variance falls ~1/N sampling rate **up to the microstructure-noise floor** (5-min for liquid US equities; finer for crypto). 5×/day is at the *very coarse* end — far below the noise floor — so uplift is monotonically positive for every RV variable. 2024 HF stochastic-vol work on E-mini S&P 500 futures confirms HAR models improve with higher-frequency data. **Foundational HAR chain:** Corsi (2009 JFE) introduces HAR; Andersen-Bollerslev-Diebold (2007 REStat) extend with jumps ("Roughing It Up"); Patton-Sheppard (2015 REStat) decompose signed-jump persistence. **Microstructure-noise boundary:** Hansen-Lunde (2006 JBES), Zhang-Mykland-Aït-Sahalia (2005 JASA TSRV), Barndorff-Nielsen et al. (2008 Ecta realized kernels), and Aït-Sahalia-Mykland-Zhang (2005 RFS) jointly define the optimal-sampling frontier; 5×/day sits safely above the noise floor for all Top-28 assets. **FX microstructure:** Krohn-Sushko (2024 JF) document intraday dynamics of global FX order flow and fixings — directly supports R04 FX RV uplift. **Direct cite for:** R01–R05 (all RV rows, IUS 3 across single-stock / index / commodity / FX / crypto), R16–R20 (VRP, IUS 2), R21 (cross-asset realized correlation, IUS 2). **Strongest single justification for the top of Ranking B and Ranking B-ES.**

(2008 Ecta realized kernels), and Aït-Sahalia-Mykland-Zhang (2005 RFS) jointly define the optimal-sampling frontier; 5×/day sits safely above the noise floor for all Top-28 assets. **FX microstructure:** Krohn-Sushko (2024 JF) document intraday dynamics of global FX order flow and fixings — directly supports R04 FX RV uplift. **Direct cite for:** R01–R05 (all RV rows, IUS 3 across single-stock / index / commodity / FX / crypto), R16–R20 (VRP, IUS 2), R21 (cross-asset realized correlation, IUS 2). **Strongest single justification for the top of Ranking B and Ranking B-ES.**

### 5.3 VPIN / order-flow toxicity (Easley-López de Prado-O'Hara 2012 RFS; Abad-Yagüe 2018 JIFMIM; arxiv 2508.06788 2025 OFI S&P 500 E-mini) — Grade A

VPIN computes flow imbalance over volume buckets; shown to lead the May 2010 Flash Crash by ~30 min. The signal is *defined* on intraday volume — EOD cannot reproduce it. 2025 arxiv work on S&P 500 E-mini at 1-second frequency reinforces the OFI→return link and shows macro-news announcements reshape the dynamic. **Engagement:** canonical VPIN uses 50-bucket aggregations within the day; 5×/day captures only ~5 reads, so direction preserved with reduced power. **Direct cite for:** T10 (VPIN single-stock, IUS 3), T11 (VPIN index, IUS 3), T12 (VPIN commodity, IUS 2), K01 (BTC/ETH order imbalance, IUS 3). Med-whipsaw flag reflects 5×/day coarseness vs canonical 50-bucket VPIN.

input.old_string:
### 5.4 Options-implied information (Cremers-Weinbaum 2010 JFQA; An-Ang-Bali-Cakici 2014 JF; MDPI FRM 2024 17/1/39) — Grade A

Put-call IV spreads predict next-day stock returns at t > 4, decay in 1–2 days (CW 2010). An et al. (2014) jointly characterize stock-option return predictability. **2024 MDPI study is the most recent single-stock-specific evidence:** implements intraday model-free IV at 1m/10m/60m windows and directly validates the return-vol relationship at those frequencies. **⚠ Single-asset-evidence note:** CW 2010 and An et al. 2014 are both US single-stock only — the mechanism is argued to generalize to index/commodity/crypto/FX skew but IUS is capped at 2 for those extrapolated rows (R12–R15). **Direct cite for:** R11 (single-stock skew, IUS 3), R08 (single-name IV, IUS 3), R12–R15 (skew extrapolations, IUS 2, all ⚠).
input.new_string:
### 5.4 Options-implied information (Cremers-Weinbaum 2010 JFQA; An-Ang-Bali-Cakici 2014 JF; MDPI FRM 2024 17/1/39) — Grade A

Put-call IV spreads predict next-day stock returns at t > 4, decay in 1–2 days (CW 2010). An et al. (2014) jointly characterize stock-option return predictability. **2024 MDPI study is the most recent single-stock-specific evidence:** implements intraday model-free IV at 1m/10m/60m windows and directly validates the return-vol relationship at those frequencies. **Intraday VRP:** Papagelis (2025 WP) constructs an intraday variance-risk-premium series, showing the VRP rises at the open and decays into the close — so 5×/day captures within-day VRP regime shifts that the canonical daily VRP obscures. **Market-skewness pricing:** Chang-Christoffersen-Jacobs (2013 JFE) link the cross-section of stock returns to market-skewness risk loadings — reinforces R11 equity-skew intraday relevance. **PEAD / attention channel:** Kaczmarek-Zaremba (2025 FinResLett v86, reviving PEAD via ML) + Garfinkel-Hribar-Hsiao (2024 SSRN 5040374 CNN-on-earnings) + DellaVigna-Pollet (2009 JF) + Livnat-Mendenhall (2006 JAR) + Da-Engelberg-Gao (2011 JF search-attention) + Jiang-Li-Wang (2021 RFS) together support C01 earnings-window intraday uplift. **⚠ Single-asset-evidence note:** CW 2010 and An et al. 2014 are both US single-stock only — the mechanism is argued to generalize to index/commodity/crypto/FX skew but IUS is capped at 2 for those extrapolated rows (R12–R15). **Direct cite for:** R11 (single-stock skew, IUS 3), R08 (single-name IV, IUS 3), R12–R15 (skew extrapolations, IUS 2, all ⚠).

### 5.5 ETF flow intraday signal (Ben-David-Franzoni-Moussawi 2018 JF; Brown-Davies-Ringgenberg 2021 JFE; Lazo-Paz 2024 AEA; 2024–25 BTC-ETF flow papers) — Grade A equity, Grade B crypto

input.old_string:
### 5.5 ETF flow intraday signal (Ben-David-Franzoni-Moussawi 2018 JF; Brown-Davies-Ringgenberg 2021 JFE; Lazo-Paz 2024 AEA; 2024–25 BTC-ETF flow papers) — Grade A equity, Grade B crypto

BFM 2018 documents ETF arbitrage producing intraday return-predictability via creation/redemption observable through premium/discount + AP volume. Lazo-Paz (2024 AEA) builds an ETF-based stock-price-fragility measure. Creation predicts next-day positive returns **conditional on a premium** (not symmetric). **Engagement:** flow is intraday-inferable; canonical NAV is daily. 5×/day proxy captures AP-arb signal EOD reports miss. Med-cost flag reflects difficulty of building the proxy (premium/discount + AP volume) from public data. **Direct cite for:** R33 (equity ETF flow, IUS 2), R34 (BTC/ETH ETF flow, IUS 2).
